home.emptyTeacher: No courses yet. Create your first course to get started.
home.emptyStudent: You haven't been added to a course yet. Ask your teacher for an invite link.
home.unreadNotesTooltip_one: A teacher left {{count}} new note on one of your conversations
home.unreadNotesTooltip_other: A teacher left {{count}} new notes across your conversations

create.title: Create Course
create.semesterHint: Use VT (spring) or HT (autumn) plus a 4-digit year, e.g. VT2026, HT2026.

teacherGuide.title: Set up Minerva for your course
teacherGuide.intro: Minerva is an AI study assistant that answers from your own course materials. Your students reach it from a link inside your Moodle course, and setting it up takes about ten minutes.
teacherGuide.adminNote: Your administrator has registered Minerva as an external tool across Moodle. You still need to enable it for each of your courses (step 2) before it appears in the activity chooser.

login.title: 1. Log in to Minerva
login.body: Go to minerva.dsv.su.se and sign in with your SU account. Courses imported nightly from Daisy appear on your dashboard. If a course is missing, create it with New Course.
login.alt: Minerva dashboard showing the teacher's courses

enableTool.title: 2. Enable Minerva in your course
enableTool.body: In your Moodle course, open the More menu and choose LTI External tools. Find Minerva, activate it, and turn on Show in activity chooser.
enableTool.alt: Course LTI External tools page with Minerva activated and shown in the activity chooser
enableTool.body2: Once enabled correctly, Minerva appears in the activity chooser with its logo. If you instead see a blank or generic icon, the tool was registered without its icon; contact lambda@dsv.su.se.

addActivity.title: 3. Add the Minerva activity to a section
addActivity.body: In your Moodle course, turn on Edit mode, select Add an activity or resource in the section where you want the assistant, and choose Minerva.
addActivity.alt: Moodle activity chooser with the Minerva tool and its logo
addActivity.body2: Give it a name students will recognise, then choose Save and return to course. The activity now appears in your course.
addActivity.altConfig: External tool settings with the activity name filled in
addActivity.altCourse: Moodle course page with the Minerva activity added

linkMaterials.title: 4. Connect your course materials
linkMaterials.body: Open Minerva settings from the course's More menu, pick your matching Minerva course from the list, and choose Link Minerva course.
linkMaterials.alt: Minerva settings link form with the course dropdown
linkMaterials.body2: Then choose Sync materials to upload your files, pages, and section summaries so the assistant can answer from them. Re-run it whenever you change materials; it also runs automatically twice an hour.
linkMaterials.altLinked: Linked course with Sync materials and Unlink buttons

firstLaunch.title: 5. Open it once to connect the activity
firstLaunch.body: Click the Minerva activity you added. The first time, pick which Minerva course it should open and choose Link and open Minerva. Minerva remembers this, so every later launch by you or your students opens the right course.
firstLaunch.alt: First-launch screen linking the Moodle activity to a Minerva course

result.title: That is it: here is what students see
result.body: Students click the same activity and get a chat assistant that answers from your course materials, right inside Moodle. The suggested questions are drawn from what you synced.
result.altChat: Minerva chat with suggested starter questions
result.altInMoodle: Minerva chat embedded inside the Moodle activity

tips.title: Good to know
tips.materials: The assistant only uses materials you sync. Student conversations are visible to course staff; see Data handling for details.
tips.resync: Re-sync after you change materials. You can also include teacher-answered forum threads from Minerva settings (optional; student names are removed before upload).
tips.supportLead: Questions or need higher usage limits? Email

whatWeStore.heading: What we store
whatWeStore.identity: Your identity from Shibboleth or LTI: your SU username (eppn) and display name. External-invite users see pseudonymized names of others in the course.
whatWeStore.conversations: Your conversations, messages, and any feedback you submit on AI answers.
whatWeStore.materials: Course materials uploaded by teachers or synced from Canvas / Moodle.

staffAccess.heading: Course staff can read your conversations
staffAccess.body: Teachers and TAs in your course can see every conversation you have there. Other students can't, unless a teacher explicitly shares one with the class.

whereMessagesGo.heading: Where your messages go
whereMessagesGo.cerebras: Chat messages and relevant course-material excerpts are sent to Cerebras for AI inference.
whereMessagesGo.openai: If your course's teacher has enabled OpenAI embeddings, your questions are additionally sent to OpenAI to compute a search vector. Otherwise embeddings run locally on the Minerva server.
whereMessagesGo.nothingElse: Nothing else leaves the Minerva server.

retention.heading: How long we keep it
retention.body: Conversations and messages are retained for as long as the course exists.

integrations.heading: Canvas / Moodle / Play / Daisy integrations (teachers)
integrations.canvas: Canvas: Minerva pulls course files and page content only (no submissions, rosters, or grades). Your Canvas API token is stored in the Minerva database in plaintext; revoke it in Minerva or Canvas to disconnect.
integrations.moodle: Moodle plugin: pushes course materials (files, page/book/label HTML, URLs) and a list of enrolled students (eppn + display name) to Minerva.
integrations.lti: LTI launches: when Moodle or Canvas launches Minerva in an iframe, the platform passes the student's identifier and (if configured) display name to Minerva. A user row is created on first launch.
integrations.play: Play (play.dsv.su.se): when a `.url` document points at Play, the VTT transcript is fetched and stored as searchable text inside the course.
integrations.daisy: Daisy (daisy.dsv.su.se): a nightly job lists DSV course offerings for the current and next semester and creates the matching Minerva courses. For each course it pulls the public list of teaching staff (name and the SU/DSV usernames Daisy publishes for them), turns each username into a Minerva eppn, and adds those people as course teachers. Daisy lists multiple historical logins per person; secondary ones are stored as eppn aliases so a future login under any of them resolves to the same Minerva user. No student rosters or grades are read.

acknowledgements.title: Acknowledgements
acknowledgements.intro: Minerva is made possible by contributions from people beyond the core dev team. Thank you.

accessibility.title: Accessibility statement
accessibility.intro: Stockholm University, Department of Computer and Systems Sciences (DSV), is responsible for Minerva. We want as many people as possible to be able to use it, and this statement describes how Minerva meets the Act on the Accessibility of Digital Public Service, known shortcomings, and how you can report problems.
accessibility.complianceStatus: Minerva is partially compliant with the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.1 level AA, owing to the shortcomings described below.
accessibility.shortcomingsIntro: The following content is not yet fully accessible:

shortcomings.contrast: Colour contrast across the full theme palette (light and dark) has not yet been verified against all WCAG contrast thresholds; some secondary text or interface borders may fall short in places.
shortcomings.knowledgeGraph: The teacher knowledge-graph view is an interactive canvas visualisation. A text summary and a list of relationships are provided as an alternative, but the visual graph itself cannot be fully explored with a keyboard or screen reader.
shortcomings.thirdParty: Content embedded from or launched via third parties (for example LTI launches from Moodle or Canvas, and video transcripts from play.dsv.su.se) is outside our control and may not meet the accessibility requirements.

accessibility.testingBody: We use automated accessibility testing (eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y, axe-core, and pa11y) in our development and continuous-integration pipeline, complemented by manual review against WCAG 2.1 AA. Minerva was most recently assessed on 22 May 2026.
accessibility.feedbackBody: If you find problems that are not described on this page, or believe we are not meeting the requirements, let us know so we can fix it. Contact us at <email>{{email}}</email>.
accessibility.enforcementBody: The Agency for Digital Government (DIGG) is responsible for supervising the Act on the Accessibility of Digital Public Service. If you are not satisfied with how we handle your feedback, you can <digg>report it to DIGG</digg>.
